Question: Name of Digital Solution
Response: MIESC - Multi-layer Intelligent Evaluation for Smart Contracts

Question: Description
Response: MIESC is an open-source multi-agent framework for comprehensive smart contract security analysis. It integrates 35 analysis modules across 9 defense layers (static analysis, dynamic testing, symbolic execution, formal verification, AI/LLM analysis, pattern detection, DeFi-specific analysis, exploit validation, consensus) with multi-provider LLM support (Claude, GPT, Ollama local) and automated remediation. Achieves 95.8% recall on SmartBugs-curated (137/143 contracts) and 92.5% on an EVMBench local high-severity extraction (120 findings; the official benchmark lists 117). Available on PyPI (pip install miesc) and Docker (ghcr.io/fboiero/miesc).

Question: Which SDGs
Response: SDG 9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure; SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions

Question: How is it relevant
Response: SDG 9: MIESC directly supports Target 9.5 by providing open-source blockchain security technology, democratizing access to advanced tools previously available only to well-funded organizations. SDG 16: Contributes to Target 16.5 (reduce corruption) and Target 16.6 (effective institutions) by providing transparent, verifiable security auditing for blockchain-based financial systems.

Question: Right to redistribute
Response: Yes. Original work by Fernando Boiero under AGPL-3.0. Third-party dependencies use compatible open-source licenses.

Question: Core Technologies
Response: Python 3.12+, Solidity Compiler, Slither, Mythril, Echidna, Foundry, Halmos, Aderyn, SMTChecker, Certora, Ollama, Claude API, OpenAI API, Django REST Framework, ChromaDB, MCP, JSON, SARIF

Question: Formats
Response: JSON, API, CSV, HTML, Markdown, YAML, PDF

Question: Evidence
Response: REST API with OpenAPI 3.0 spec, SARIF export, CLI with multiple output formats. See: https://github.com/fboiero/MIESC/blob/main/docs/openapi.yaml

Question: Evidence

Question: Highlights
Response: https://github.com/fboiero/MIESC, PyPI: pip install miesc, Docker: ghcr.io/fboiero/miesc:5.4.1, 9-layer defense architecture, 35 analysis modules, 95.8% recall SmartBugs, 92.5% recall on EVMBench local extraction (contextual, not an official leaderboard result), 2 academic papers, plugin system for community detectors, $0 with local models
